title: Standard PCR protocol
steps: For a 25 µL reaction volume use the following recipe: 14.9 µL dH 2 O 5 µL buffer 1.5 µL MgCl 2 1 µL FWD primer (from a 10 micromolar (µM) working solution) 1 µL REV primer (from a 10 micromolar (µM) working solution) 0.5 µL dNTPs (from a 10 micromolar (µM) mix) 0.1 µL Taq polymerase

Prepare the master mix On ice allowing for 10% pipetting error (eg. if carrying out 10 reactions, calculate the master mix quantities needed for 11 reactions). Remember to include a negative control with no DNA template and, if possible, a positive control using a known DNA template.
Aliquot 24 µL of master mix into each 0.2 mL PCR tube.
Add 1 µL of either DNA template, dH 2 O (negative control) or known DNA (positive control) to the PCR tubes and place in a thermocycler.
Extension at 72 °C for 00:02:00 go to step #5.2 x 29
Store PCR products at 4 °C , or at -20 °C for long term storage.
Check if the PCR was successful by gel electrophoresis. Gel electrophoresis by Victoria LN Jackson
creator: Victoria LN Jackson
